# Verbum

> Verbum is a free, open-source Bible study app combining 12 translations
> across 5 languages (English, Portuguese, Spanish, French, German), 344K
> cross-references from OpenBible, interlinear Hebrew and Greek with
> Strong's tagging, 62K manually-labelled emotional sentiment annotations
> in PT and ES, AI-assisted verse explanations, biblical maps, and
> topical/thematic indexes. No paywall, no advertising, MIT-licensed
> source. https://verbum-app-bible.web.app

The full source code and dataset live at
https://github.com/DavidKGBR/verbum.

## Pages

- [Home](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/): Daily verse, translation overview, getting started.
- [About](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/about): Why the project exists and the human/AI partnership behind it.
- [Reader](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/reader): Bible reader with five modes — single, parallel, immersive 3D, interlinear (Greek/Hebrew with Strong's), and structural literary view.
- [Search](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/search): Full-text search across all 12 translations.
- [Compare](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/compare): Side-by-side comparison of any verse across up to 12 translations.
- [Dictionary](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/dictionary): 14,800 entries from Easton's and Smith's Bible dictionaries.
- [Word Study](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/word-study): Strong's-keyed deep dive on Hebrew/Greek terms.
- [Map](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/map): Interactive map of 1,600 biblical places with coordinates.
- [Timeline](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/timeline): 4,000 events across the Old and New Testaments.
- [Topics](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/topics): 20,000 topical tags from Nave's Topical Bible.
- [People](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/people): 3,000 named individuals from Genesis to Revelation.
- [Places](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/places): 1,600 cities, regions, mountains, and bodies of water.
- [Authors](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/authors): The human authors of Scripture with biographies and writing context.
- [Emotional Landscape](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/emotional): Per-verse sentiment polarity and per-book emotional arcs.
- [Connections](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/connections): 344K cross-references rendered as arc diagram, semantic graph, and intertextual citations.
- [Concepts](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/concepts): Semantic threads and Hebrew→Greek genealogy of theological vocabulary.
- [Structure](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/structure): Chiasms and literary parallelism.
- [Privacy](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/privacy): LGPD/GDPR-compliant data policy.

## Blog

- [Why Verbum exists](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/blog/why-verbum-exists): Project manifesto, what's inside, and why everything is free.
- [How we manually labelled 62,209 verses for sentiment in Portuguese and Spanish](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/blog/sentiment-labels-pt-es): Methodology for the emotional landscape dataset.
- [A practical guide to reading interlinear Hebrew and Greek](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/blog/interlinear-strongs-explained): Tutorial on Strong's numbers and the interlinear reader.
- [Building Verbum with Claude as pair programmer](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/blog/built-with-claude): Honest postmortem of AI-assisted full-stack software development.
- [Twelve translations, five languages, one comparison view](https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/blog/twelve-translations): Why these specific translations were chosen.

## API

Base URL: `https://verbum-app-bible.web.app/api/v1`

Free, unauthenticated, JSON. Highlights:

- `GET /verses/search?q=love&translation=kjv&limit=20` — full-text search
- `GET /reader/page?translation=kjv&book=JHN&chapter=1` — read a chapter
- `GET /reader/parallel?book=JHN&chapter=1&translations=kjv,nvi,rvr` — parallel
- `GET /verses/{verse_id}` — single verse
- `GET /lexicon/{strongs_id}` — Strong's entry
- `GET /lexicon/interlinear?book=JHN&chapter=1` — interlinear words
- `GET /crossrefs/{verse_id}` — cross-references for a verse
- `GET /places` and `GET /places/geo` — biblical places, optionally as GeoJSON
- `GET /people` — biblical people
- `GET /emotional/landscape?book=PSA` — sentiment data per chapter

## Licensing

- Source code: MIT (https://github.com/DavidKGBR/verbum/blob/main/LICENSE)
- Bible translations: each translation's own license — KJV/BBE/ASV/WEB/Darby (public domain), NVI/RA/ACF/RVR/APEE (free public use), Luther 1545 (public domain), Neue Genfer Übersetzung (free distribution).
- Cross-references: OpenBible.info CC-BY 4.0
- Strong's lexicon, Easton's, Smith's: public domain
- STEPBible interlinear: CC-BY 4.0
- Manual sentiment labels: CC-BY 4.0 (Verbum project, April 2026)
